text: A new report shows a patient's cancer has recurred. Both the supervisor and patient are unaware. What is the most appropriate next step?
options: Inform your supervisor and prepare together to share the news.
Ask the supervisor to tell the patient alone.
Ask the patient how they feel about their health first.
rationale: Ethics: Informing the supervisor ensures professional alignment before breaking bad news to the patient [cite: 12-21].

text: Which of the following factors most increases a patient's risk of dying by suicide?
rationale: Dx: Previous suicide attempts are the single strongest predictor of completed suicide [cite: 22-28].

text: A patient on antipsychotics presents with confusion, fever (39°C), high BP, and muscle rigidity. Which investigation confirms the diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Neuroleptic Malignant Syndrome (NMS). [cite_start]Elevated CK and rigidity are diagnostic markers [cite: 29-37].

text: A 70-year-old with acute knee pain, joint effusion, and nodular deposits on the pinnae and hands. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Gout. [cite_start]Nodular deposits (tophi) and acute monoarthritis are classic for tophaceous gout [cite: 63-67].

text: A 26-year-old twisted her knee playing soccer; immediate swelling and pain. Likely diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: ACL tear often occurs during sports with a twisting motion and rapid effusion [cite: 68-73].

text: A 4-year-old with fever, runny nose, and inspiratory stridor. Likely diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Croup (Laryngotracheobronchitis) presents with stridor and a barking cough in young children [cite: 106-111].

text: A 50-year-old woman with groin swelling after coughing, now with vomiting and tenderness. Next step?
rationale: Tx: Strangulated hernia requires immediate surgical intervention to prevent bowel necrosis [cite: 112-116].

text: A fetus is diagnosed with spina bifida. Which maternal medication most likely contributed?
rationale: Risk: Valproate is a known teratogen associated with neural tube defects [cite: 168-172].

text: A 65-year-old with microcytic anemia (Hgb 85). Best investigation?
rationale: Dx: In elderly patients, iron-deficiency anemia is colon cancer until proven otherwise by colonoscopy [cite: 173-177].

text: A patient with high BP (155/95) and low Potassium (2.3). Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Primary aldosteronism presents with the triad of HTN, hypokalemia, and alkalosis [cite: 201-212].

text: A 23-year-old with hematuria during a febrile sore throat. Likely diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: IgA Nephropathy (Berger disease) causes hematuria concurrent with a respiratory infection [cite: 228-235].

text: A patient on risperidone pacing aimlessly and cannot sit still. Initial management?
rationale: Tx: Akathisia is managed by reducing the dose of the offending antipsychotic [cite: 286-291].

text: Ear pain, fever, and pinna pushed forward with tenderness behind the ear. Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Acute Mastoiditis is a complication of Otitis Media requiring urgent attention [cite: 380-384].
